pattern: With such a small array, checking every pair directly against both conditions -- equal values and a divisible index product -- is simpler and just as reliable as any cleverer bookkeeping.
hints: The array is small enough that comparing every pair of indices directly is fast enough; no fancy structure is required.
For each pair, first check that the two values match, since that filters out most pairs immediately.
Only for the pairs that pass the value check, test whether the product of their indices is a multiple of k.

edge_inputs.description: single element array has no pairs
k equal to 1 means every pair with equal values qualifies
a pair involving index 0 always has a product of zero, divisible by any k
equal values whose index product is never divisible by a larger k
k at the maximum bound with many repeated values
maximum-bound values repeated across a short array

stress.description: random arrays with a small value alphabet to encourage repeats
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [nums, k]
